mut users = self.users.write().await;\n        users.insert(user_id.into(), UserCredentials {\n            password: password.into(),\n            display_name: display_name.into(),\n self.add_user(\"admin\", \"admin123\", \"Administrator\", UserRole::Admin).await;\n        // Default operator: operator/operator123\n        self.add_user(\"operator\", \"operator123\", \"Operator User\", UserRole::Operator).await;\n        // Default viewer: viewer/viewer123\n        self.add_user(\"viewer\", \"viewer123\", \"Viewer User\", UserRole::Viewer).await;\n        self\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for SimpleAuth {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl AuthProvider for SimpleAuth {\n    async fn authenticate(&self, user_id: &str, credentials: &str) -> Result<AuthResult, ServerError> {\n        let users = self.users.read().await;\n\n        let user = users.get(user_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| ServerError::AuthenticationFailed(format!(\"user not found: {}\", user_id)))?;\n\n        if user.password != credentials {\n            return Err(ServerError::AuthenticationFailed(\"invalid credentials\".to_string()));\n        }\n\n        Ok(AuthResult {\n            user_id: user_id.to_string(),\n            display_name: user.display_name.clone(),\n            role: user.role,\n }\n}\n\n/// Actions that require permission checks.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum PermissionAction {\n    View,\n    SpawnWorkers,\n    KillWorkers,\n    AssignBeads,\n    ModifyConfig,\n    ManageUsers,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_simple_auth() {\n        let auth = SimpleAuth::default();\n FORGE Server - Team collaboration and multi-user session support.\n//!\n//! This crate provides:\n//! - Session management for multiple connected users\n//! - Role-based access control (RBAC)\n//! - WebSocket server for real-time updates\n//! - HTTP API for FORGE operations\n//! - Bead assignment tracking\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//!\n//! The server runs alongside the FORGE TUI, enabling:\n//! - Multiple users to observe the same FORGE instance\n//! - Named user sessions with attribution on actions\n//! - Shared bead queue with assignment capabilities\n//! - Real-time state synchronization via WebSocket\n\npub mod auth;\npub mod session;\npub mod websocket;\npub mod assignment;\npub mod protocol;\npub mod client;\n\npub use session::{SessionManager, SessionRegistry};\npub use assignment::BeadAssignmentTracker;\npub use auth::{AuthProvider, AuthResult, SimpleAuth};\npub use protocol::{ServerMessage, ClientMessage, StateUpdate, ServerState};\npub use websocket::{ForgeServer, ServerConfig, create_server};\npub use client::{ForgeClient, ClientConfig, ClientStateSnapshot, ConnectedUser};\n\nuse forge_core::ForgeError;\n\n/// FORGE server error type.\n#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum ServerError {\n    #[error(\"authentication failed: {0}\")]\n    AuthenticationFailed(String),\n\n    #[error(\"permission denied: {0}\")]\n    PermissionDenied(String),\n\n    #[error(\"session not found: {0}\")]\n    SessionNotFound(String),\n\n    #[error(\"invalid request: {0}\")]\n    InvalidRequest(String),\n\n    #[error(\"server error: {0}\")]\n    ServerError(String),\n\n    #[error(\"IO error: {0}\")]\n    Io(#[from] std::io::Error),\n\n    #[error(\"JSON error: {0}\")]\n    Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n}\n\nimpl From<ServerError> for ForgeError {\n    fn from(err: ServerError) -> Self {\n        ForgeError::Internal { message: err.to_string() }\n    }\n}\n","numLines":64,"startLine":1,"totalLines":64}}}

Session management for FORGE multi-user support.\n//!\n//! Manages active user sessions, tracks connections, and provides session lookup.\n\nuse crate::ServerError;\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// Session manager for tracking active user sessions.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct SessionManager {\n    sessions: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, UserSession>>>,\n    user_sessions: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, Vec<String>>>>, // user_id -> session_ids\n}\n\nimpl SessionManager {\n    /// Create a new session manager.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            sessions: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            user_sessions: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new session for a user.\n    pub async fn create_session(\n        &self,\n        user_id: impl Into<String>,\n        display_name: impl Into<String>,\n        role: UserRole,\n    ) -> Result<UserSession, ServerError> {\n        let session_id = Uuid::new_v4().to_string();\n        let user_id = user_id.into();\n        let display_name = display_name.into();\n\n        let session = UserSession::new(\n            &session_id,\n            &user_id,\n            &display_name,\n            role,\n        );\n\n        // Add to sessions map\n        {\n            let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n            sessions.insert(session_id.clone(), session.clone());\n        }\n\n        // Add to user_sessions map\n        {\n            let mut user_sessions = self.user_sessions.write().await;\n            user_sessions.entry(user_id.clone())\n                .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n                .push(session_id.clone());\n        }\n\n        Ok(session)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a session by ID.\n    pub async fn get_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<UserSession> {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        sessions.get(session_id).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Remove a session (user disconnect).\n    pub async fn remove_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<UserSession> {\n        let session = {\n            let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n            sessions.remove(session_id)\n        };\n\n        if let Some(ref session) = session {\n            // Remove from user_sessions\n            let mut user_sessions = self.user_sessions.write().await;\n            if let Some(ids) = user_sessions.get_mut(&session.user_id) {\n                ids.retain(|id| id != session_id);\n                if ids.is_empty() {\n                    user_sessions.remove(&session.user_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        session\n    }\n\n    /// Update session activity.\n    pub async fn update_activity(&self, session_id: &str) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        let session = sessions.get_mut(session_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| ServerError::SessionNotFound(session_id.to_string()))?;\n        session.update_activity();\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update session's current view.\n    pub async fn update_view(&self, session_id: &str, view: impl Into<String>) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.write().await;\n        let session = sessions.get_mut(session_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| ServerError::SessionNotFound(session_id.to_string()))?;\n        session.set_view(view);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get all active sessions.\n    pub async fn all_sessions(&self) -> Vec<UserSession> {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        sessions.values().cloned().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Get count of active sessions.\n    pub async fn session_count(&self) -> usize {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        sessions.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Get sessions for a specific user.\n    pub async fn user_sessions(&self, user_id: &str) -> Vec<UserSession> {\n        let session_ids = {\n            let user_sessions = self.user_sessions.read().await;\n            user_sessions.get(user_id).cloned().unwrap_or_default()\n        };\n\n        let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n        session_ids.iter()\n            .filter_map(|id| sessions.get(id).cloned())\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up stale sessions (no activity for 1 hour).\n    pub async fn cleanup_stale(&self) -> Vec<UserSession> {\n        const STALE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: i64 = 3600; // 1 hour\n\n        let stale_ids = {\n            let sessions = self.sessions.read().await;\n            sessions.iter()\n                .filter(|(_, s)| s.is_stale(STALE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS))\n                .map(|(id, _)| id.clone())\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n        };\n\n        let mut removed = Vec::new();\n        for session_id in stale_ids {\n            if let Some(session) = self.remove_session(&session_id).await {\n                removed.push(session);\n            }\n        }\n\n        removed\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a user has permission based on their session role.\n    pub async fn check_permission(&self, session_id: &str, action: super::auth::PermissionAction) -> Result<bool, ServerError> {\n        let session = self.get_session(session_id).await\n            .ok_or_else(|| ServerError::SessionNotFound(session_id.to_string()))?;\n\n        Ok(super::auth::check_permission(session.role, action))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for SessionManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session registry for tracking session metadata across the server.\npub struct SessionRegistry {\n    manager: SessionManager,\n    connected_at: std::time::Instant,\n}\n\nimpl SessionRegistry {\n    /// Create a new session registry.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            manager: SessionManager::new(),\n            connected_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the session manager.\n    pub fn manager(&self) -> &SessionManager {\n        &self.manager\n    }\n\n    /// Get server uptime.\n    pub fn uptime(&self) -> std::time::Duration {\n        self.connected_at.elapsed()\n    }\n\n    /// Get server statistics.\n    pub async fn stats(&self) -> SessionStats {\n        let sessions = self.manager.all_sessions().await;\n\n        let viewers = sessions.iter().filter(|s| s.role == UserRole::Viewer).count();\n        let operators = sessions.iter().filter(|s| s.role == UserRole::Operator).count();\n        let admins = sessions.iter().filter(|s| s.role == UserRole::Admin).count();\n\n        SessionStats {\n            total_sessions: sessions.len(),\n            viewers,\n            operators,\n            admins,\n            uptime: self.uptime(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for SessionRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Session statistics.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SessionStats {\n    pub total_sessions: usize,\n    pub viewers: usize,\n    pub operators: usize,\n    pub admins: usize,\n    pub uptime: std::time::Duration,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_session_creation() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        let session = manager.create_session(\"user1\", \"User One\", UserRole::Operator).await.unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(session.user_id, \"user1\");\n        assert_eq!(session.display_name, \"User One\");\n        assert_eq!(session.role, UserRole::Operator);\n        assert!(!session.is_stale(3600)); // 1 hour timeout\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_session_retrieval() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        manager.create_session(\"user1\", \"User One\", UserRole::Operator).await.unwrap();\n\n        let sessions = manager.all_sessions().await;\n        assert_eq!(sessions.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(sessions[0].user_id, \"user1\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_session_removal() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        let session = manager.create_session(\"user1\", \"User One\", UserRole::Operator).await.unwrap();\n\n assert_eq!(manager.session_count().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_session_activity() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        let session_id = {\n            let session = manager.create_session(\"user1\", \"User One\", UserRole::Operator).await.unwrap();\n            session.session_id.clone()\n        };\n\n        manager.update_activity(&session_id).await.unwrap();\n\n        let session = manager.get_session(&session_id).await.unwrap();\n        // Session should still be active (just updated)\n        assert!(!session.is_stale(3600)); // 1 hour timeout\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_permission_check() {\n        use crate::auth::PermissionAction;\n\n        let manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        let viewer_session = manager.create_session(\"viewer\", \"Viewer\", UserRole::Viewer).await.unwrap();\n        let admin_session = manager.create_session(\"admin\", \"Admin\", UserRole::Admin).await.unwrap();\n\n        // Viewer can't spawn workers\n        let can_spawn = manager.check_permission(&viewer_session.session_id, PermissionAction::SpawnWorkers).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(!can_spawn);\n\n        // Admin can spawn workers\n        let can_spawn = manager.check_permission(&admin_session.session_id, PermissionAction::SpawnWorkers).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(can_spawn);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_multiple_sessions_per_user() {\n        let manager = assignment\n    user_assignments: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, Vec<String>>>>, // user_id -> bead_ids\n}\n\nimpl BeadAssignmentTracker {\n    /// Create a new assignment tracker.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            assignments: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            user_assignments: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Assign a bead self.assignments.read().await;\n            if let Some(existing) = assignments.get(&bead_id) {\n                if existing.assigned_to.as_deref() == Some(&to_user) {\n                    return  // Add to assignments\n        {\n            let mut assignments = self.assignments.write().await;\n            assignments.insert(bead_id.clone(), assignment.clone());\n        }\n\n        // Add to user_assignments\n        {\n            let mut user_assignments = self.user_assignments.write().await;\n            user_assignments.entry(to_user.clone())\n                .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n                .push(bead_id.clone());\n        }\n\n        Ok(assignment)\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign a bead.\n    pub async fn unassign(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) assignments.remove(&bead_id)\n        };\n\n        if let Some(ref assignment) = assignment {\n            // Remove from user_assignments\n            if let Some(ref assigned_to) =         }\n        }\n\n        Ok(assignment)\n    }\n\n    /// Get assignment for a bead.\n    pub async fn get_assignment(&self, bead_id: &str) -> Option<BeadAssignment> {\n        let assignments };\n\n        let assignments = self.assignments.read().await;\n        bead_ids.iter()\n            .filter_map(|id| assignments.get(id).cloned())\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Get all /// Reassign a bead from one user to another.\n    pub async fn reassign(\n        &self,\n        bead_id: impl Into<String>,\n        from_user: impl Into<String>,\n        to_user: impl Into<String>,\n        by_user: impl Into<String>,\n    ) -> Result<BeadAssignment> {\n        let bead_id = bead_id.into();\n        let from_user = from_user.into();\n\n        // Verify current )\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Unassign from old user\n        self.unassign(&bead_id).await?;\n\n        // Assign to new user\n        self.assign(&bead_id, to_user, by_user).await\n    }\n\n    /// Get count of assignments per user.\n    pub async fn assignment_counts(&self) -> HashMap<String, usize> {\n        let user_assignments WebSocket client for connecting to a remote FORGE server.\n//!\n//! This module provides the client functionality for connecting to a FORGE\n//! server in multi-user collaborative mode.\n\nuse super::protocol::{ServerMessage, ClientMessage, StateUpdate, ServerInfo};\nuse super::ServerError;\nuse forge_core::{UserSession, UserRole};\nuse futures_util::{SinkExt, StreamExt};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock, Mutex};\nuse tokio_tungstenite::{connect_async, tungstenite::protocol::Message};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\n/// Configuration for connecting to a FORGE server.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientConfig {\n    /// Server WebSocket URL (e.g., ws://localhost:8080/ws)\n    pub server_url: String,\n    /// User ID for authentication\n    pub user_id: String,\n    /// Password for authentication\n    pub password: String,\n}\n\n/// FORGE WebSocket client.\npub struct ForgeClient {\n    config: ClientConfig,\n    /// Channel for broadcasting state updates to local subscribers\n    state_tx: broadcast::Sender<ServerMessage>,\n    /// Current state snapshot\n    current_state: Arc<RwLock<ClientState>>,\n    /// WebSocket write mutex (for sending messages)\n    write_tx: Arc<Mutex<Option<broadcast::Sender<ClientMessage>>>>,\n}\n\n/// Current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\nstruct ClientState {\n    /// Our session info\n    session: Option<UserSession>,\n    /// Server info\n    server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    /// Current state update\n    state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    /// Connected users\n    connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    /// Whether we're authenticated\n    authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n/// A connected user.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ConnectedUser {\n    pub user_id: String,\n    pub display_name: String,\n    pub role: UserRole,\n    pub current_view: Option<String>,\n    pub connected_at: chrono::DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl ForgeClient {\n    /// Create a new FORGE client.\n    pub fn new(config: ClientConfig) -> Self {\n        let (state_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(1000);\n\n        Self {\n            config,\n            state_tx,\n            current_state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(ClientState::default())),\n            write_tx: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Connect to the server and run the client loop.\n    pub async fn connect_and_run(mut self) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        let url = self.config.server_url.clone();\n        info!(\"Connecting to FORGE server: {}\", url);\n\n        let (ws_stream, _) = connect_async(&url)\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| ServerError::ServerError(format!(\"Connection failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        info!(\"Connected to server\");\n\n        let (mut ws_sender, mut ws_receiver) = ws_stream.split();\n\n        // Create channel for sending messages\n        let (write_tx, _) = broadcast::channel(100);\n        *self.write_tx.lock().await = Some(write_tx.clone());\n\n        // Subscribe to outgoing messages\n        let mut write_rx = write_tx.subscribe();\n\n        // Spawn task for sending messages\n        let _write_tx_clone = self.write_tx.clone();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            while let Ok(client_msg) = write_rx.recv().await {\n                let json = serde_json::to_string(&client_msg)\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|_| r#\"{\"error\":\"serialize failed\"}\"#.to_string());\n\n                if let Err(e) = ws_sender.send(Message::Text(json.into())).await {\n                    error!(\"Failed to send message to server: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        });\n\n        // Send authentication\n        let auth_msg = ClientMessage::Authenticate {\n            user_id: self.config.user_id.clone(),\n            credentials: self.config.password.clone(),\n        };\n        self.send_message(auth_msg).await;\n\n        // Receive messages from server\n        while let Some(msg_result) = ws_receiver.next().await {\n            match msg_result {\n                Ok(msg) => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.handle_message(msg).await {\n                        error!(\"Error handling message: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"WebSocket error: {}\", e);\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        warn!(\"Disconnected from server\");\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a message from the server.\n    async fn handle_message(&mut self, msg: Message) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            Message::Text(text) => {\n                if let Ok(server_msg) = serde_json::from_str::<ServerMessage>(&text) {\n                    self.handle_server_message(server_msg).await?;\n                }\n            }\n            Message::Close(_) => {\n                debug!(\"Server closed connection\");\n                return Err(ServerError::ServerError(\"Server closed connection\".to_string()));\n            }\n            Message::Ping(_data) => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Handle a server message.\n    async fn handle_server_message(&mut self, msg: ServerMessage) -> Result<(), ServerError> {\n        match msg {\n            ServerMessage::Welcome { session, server_info } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.session = Some(session.clone());\n                state.server_info = Some(server_info.clone());\n                state.authenticated = true;\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"Authenticated as {} ({})\", session.display_name, session.role);\n\n                // Request full state sync\n                self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n            }\n            ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update) => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.state_update = Some(update.clone());\n                drop(state);\n\n                // Broadcast to local subscribers\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(ServerMessage::StateUpdate(update));\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserJoined { ref user, ref display_name, role } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.push(ConnectedUser {\n                    user_id: user.clone(),\n                    display_name: display_name.clone(),\n                    role,\n                    current_view: None,\n                    connected_at: Utc::now(),\n                });\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} ({}) joined\", display_name, role);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::UserLeft { ref user } => {\n                let mut state = self.current_state.write().await;\n                state.connected_users.retain(|u| u.user_id != *user);\n                drop(state);\n\n                info!(\"User {} left\", user);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadAssigned { ref bead_id, ref assigned_to, ref assigned_by } => {\n                info!(\"Bead {} assigned to {} by {}\", bead_id, assigned_to, assigned_by);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::WorkerChanged { ref worker_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Worker {} status changed to {:?}\", worker_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::BeadChanged { ref bead_id, status } => {\n                debug!(\"Bead {} status changed to {:?}\", bead_id, status);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::ChatMessage { ref from, ref message, timestamp: _ } => {\n                info!(\"Chat from {}: {}\", from, message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Error { ref message } => {\n                warn!(\"Server error: {}\", message);\n                let _ = self.state_tx.send(msg);\n            }\n            ServerMessage::Ping => {\n                // Respond with pong\n                let _ = self.send_message(ClientMessage::Pong).await;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Send a message to the server.\n    async fn send_message(&self, msg: ClientMessage) {\n        if let Some(write_tx) = self.write_tx.lock().await.as_ref() {\n            let _ = write_tx.send(msg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Subscribe to state updates.\n    pub fn subscribe(&self) -> broadcast::Receiver<ServerMessage> {\n        self.state_tx.subscribe()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current state.\n    pub async fn get_state(&self) -> ClientStateSnapshot {\n        let state = self.current_state.read().await;\n        ClientStateSnapshot {\n            session: state.session.clone(),\n            server_info: state.server_info.clone(),\n            state_update: state.state_update.clone(),\n            connected_users: state.connected_users.clone(),\n            authenticated: state.authenticated,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Assign a bead to a user.\n    pub async fn assign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, to: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::AssignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            to: to.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign a bead.\n    pub async fn unassign_bead(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UnassignBead {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn a worker.\n    pub async fn spawn_worker(&self, model: impl Into<String>, count: u32) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SpawnWorker {\n            model: model.into(),\n            count,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Kill a worker.\n    pub async fn kill_worker(&self, worker_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::KillWorker {\n            worker_id: worker_id.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Change bead status.\n    pub async fn change_bead_status(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, status: forge_core::BeadStatus) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChangeBeadStatus {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            status,\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Send a chat message.\n    pub async fn send_chat(&self, message: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::ChatMessage {\n            message: message.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Update current view.\n    pub async fn update_view(&self, view: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::UpdateView {\n            view: view.into(),\n        }).await;\n    }\n\n    /// Request full state sync.\n    pub async fn request_sync(&self) {\n        self.send_message(ClientMessage::SyncState).await;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Snapshot of the current client state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClientStateSnapshot {\n    pub session: Option<UserSession>,\n    pub server_info: Option<ServerInfo>,\n    pub state_update: Option<StateUpdate>,\n    pub connected_users: Vec<ConnectedUser>,\n    pub authenticated: bool,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_client_config() {\n        let config = ClientConfig {\n            server_url: \"ws://localhost:8080/ws\".to_string(),\n            user_id: \"testuser\".to_string(),\n            password: \"testpass\".to_string(),\n        };\n\n        assert_eq!(config.server_url, \"ws://localhost:8080/ws\");\n        assert_eq!(config.user_id, \"testuser\");\n        assert_eq!(config.password, \"testpass\");\n    }\n}\n","numLines":340,"startLine":1,"totalLines":340}}}

Shared type definitions used across FORGE crates.\n//!\n//! This module provides common types that are used by multiple FORGE components,\n//! ensuring consistent representation across the codebase.\n\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\n/// Unique identifier for a worker.\npub type WorkerId = String;\n\n/// Unique identifier for a bead/task.\npub type BeadId = String;\n\n/// Worker status as reported in status files.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum WorkerStatus {\n    /// Worker is running and processing tasks\n    Active,\n    /// Worker is running but idle (no current task)\n    #[default]\n    Idle,\n    /// Worker process has failed or crashed\n    Failed,\n    /// Worker was intentionally stopped\n    Stopped,\n    /// Worker status file is corrupted or unreadable\n    Error,\n    /// Worker is starting up\n    Starting,\n    /// Worker is paused (not claiming new tasks)\n    Paused,\n}\n\nimpl WorkerStatus {\n    /// Returns true if the worker is considered healthy.\n    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Active | Self::Idle | Self::Starting | Self::Paused)\n    }\n\n    /// Returns true if the worker is paused.\n    pub fn is_paused(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Paused)\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the status indicator emoji for TUI display.\n    pub fn indicator(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Active => \"✅\",\n            Self::Idle => \"💤\",\n            Self::Failed => \"❌\",\n            Self::Stopped => \"⏹️\",\n            Self::Error => \"⚠️\",\n            Self::Starting => \"🔄\",\n            Self::Paused => \"⏸️\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for WorkerStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            Self::Idle => write!(f, \"idle\"),\n            Self::Failed => write!(f, \"failed\"),\n            Self::Stopped => write!(f, \"stopped\"),\n            Self::Error => write!(f, \"error\"),\n            Self::Starting => write!(f, \"starting\"),\n            Self::Paused => write!(f, \"paused\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Bead/task priority levels (P0 = critical, P4 = backlog).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\npub enum Priority {\n    /// Critical priority (P0) - blocking, must be done immediately\n    #[serde(rename = \"0\")]\n    P0,\n    /// High priority (P1) - important, should be done soon\n    #[serde(rename = \"1\")]\n    P1,\n    /// Normal priority (P2) - standard work\n    #[serde(rename = \"2\")]\n    #[default]\n    P2,\n    /// Low priority (P3) - nice to have\n    #[serde(rename = \"3\")]\n    P3,\n    /// Backlog (P4) - someday/maybe\n    #[serde(rename = \"4\")]\n    P4,\n}\n\nimpl Priority {\n    /// Returns the score contribution for task value scoring.\n    ///\n    /// Per ADR 0007: Priority contributes 0-40 points.\n    pub fn score(&self) -> u32 {\n        match self {\n            Self::P0 => 40,\n            Self::P1 => 30,\n            Self::P2 => 20,\n            Self::P3 => 10,\n            Self::P4 => 5,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the worker tier recommended for this priority.\n    pub fn recommended_tier(&self) -> WorkerTier {\n        match self {\n            Self::P0 | Self::P1 => WorkerTier::Premium,\n            Self::P2 => WorkerTier::Standard,\n            Self::P3 | Self::P4 => WorkerTier::Budget,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for Priority {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::P0 => write!(f, \"P0\"),\n            Self::P1 => write!(f, \"P1\"),\n            Self::P2 => write!(f, \"P2\"),\n            Self::P3 => write!(f, \"P3\"),\n            Self::P4 => write!(f, \"P4\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Worker tier for model routing.\n///\n/// Per ADR 0003, workers are grouped into tiers for cost optimization.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum WorkerTier {\n    /// Premium tier (Opus, GPT-4) - for critical/complex tasks\n    Premium,\n    /// Standard tier (Sonnet) - for most tasks\n    Standard,\n    /// Budget tier (Haiku) - for simple/low-priority tasks\n    Budget,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for WorkerTier {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Premium => write!(f, \"premium\"),\n            Self::Standard => write!(f, \"standard\"),\n            Self::Budget => write!(f, \"budget\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Bead/task status.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum BeadStatus {\n    /// Task is open and ready to be worked on\n    #[default]\n    Open,\n    /// Task is currently being worked on\n    InProgress,\n    /// Task is completed\n    Closed,\n    /// Task is blocked by dependencies\n    Blocked,\n    /// Task is deferred to a later date\n    Deferred,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for BeadStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Open => write!(f, \"open\"),\n            Self::InProgress => write!(f, \"in_progress\"),\n            Self::Closed => write!(f, \"closed\"),\n            Self::Blocked => write!(f, \"blocked\"),\n            Self::Deferred => write!(f, \"deferred\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Type of bead/issue.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum BeadType {\n    /// Generic task\n    #[default]\n    Task,\n    /// Bug fix\n    Bug,\n    /// New feature\n    Feature,\n    /// Epic (collection of related tasks)\n    Epic,\n    /// Research/investigation\n    Research,\n    /// Documentation\n    Docs,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for BeadType {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Task => write!(f, \"task\"),\n            Self::Bug => write!(f, \"bug\"),\n            Self::Feature => write!(f, \"feature\"),\n            Self::Epic => write!(f, \"epic\"),\n            Self::Research => write!(f, \"research\"),\n            Self::Docs => write!(f, \"docs\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Model identifier with tier classification.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ModelInfo {\n    /// Model identifier (e.g., \"sonnet\", \"opus\", \"haiku\")\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Display name\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Worker tier for this model\n    pub tier: WorkerTier,\n    /// Cost per million input tokens in USD\n    pub cost_per_million_input: f64,\n    /// Cost per million output tokens in USD\n    pub cost_per_million_output: f64,\n}\n\nimpl ModelInfo {\n    /// Create a new model info with default costs.\n    pub fn new(id: impl Into<String>, name: impl Into<String>, tier: WorkerTier) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: id.into(),\n            name: name.into(),\n            tier,\n            cost_per_million_input: 0.0,\n            cost_per_million_output: 0.0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set input/output costs and return self for chaining.\n    pub fn with_costs(mut self, input: f64, output: f64) -> Self {\n        self.cost_per_million_input = input;\n        self.cost_per_million_output = output;\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// Timestamp type used throughout FORGE.\npub type assert!(WorkerStatus::Starting.is_healthy());\n        assert!(!WorkerStatus::Failed.is_healthy());\n        assert!(!WorkerStatus::Stopped.is_healthy());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn  })?;\n309\t\n310\t        if let Some(assignment) = assignments.get_mut(&bead_id) {\n311\t            assignment.mark_in_progress(&user_id);\n312\t\n313\t            debug!(\n314\t                bead_id = %bead_id,\n315\t                user_id = %user_id,\n316\t                \"Marked bead as in progress\"\n317\t            );\n318\t\n319\t            // Persist if storage is configured\n320\t            if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n321\t                self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n322\t            }\n323\t\n324\t            Ok(())\n325\t        } else {\n326\t            // Create a new assignment if it doesn't exist\n327\t            let mut assignment = BeadAssignment::new(bead_id.clone());\n328\t            assignment.mark_in_progress(&user_id);\n329\t            assignments.insert(bead_id.clone(), assignment);\n330\t\n331\t            // Persist if storage is configured\n332\t            if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n333\t                self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n334\t            }\n335\t\n336\t            Ok(())\n337\t        }\n338\t    }\n339\t\n340\t    /// Mark a bead Bead assignment tracking for team collaboration.\n//!\n//! This module provides functionality for tracking which user is assigned\n//! to which bead/task, enabling coordination across multiple users.\n\nuse crate::{ForgeError, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::fs;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn};\n\n/// Assignment record for a bead.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct BeadAssignment {\n    /// Bead ID\n    pub bead_id: String,\n    /// User ID currently assigned to this bead\n    pub assigned_to: Option<String>,\n    /// When the assignment was made\n    pub assigned_at: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,\n    /// User ID who made the assignment\n    pub assigned_by: Option<String>,\n    /// Assignment priority (for team coordination)\n    pub priority: AssignmentPriority,\n    /// Assignment status\n    pub status: AssignmentStatus,\n}\n\n/// Priority level for bead assignment.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum AssignmentPriority {\n    /// Critical - needs immediate attention\n    Critical,\n    /// High - should be worked on soon\n    High,\n    /// Normal - standard priority\n    Normal,\n    /// Low - can wait\n    Low,\n}\n\nimpl AssignmentPriority {\n    /// Get the numeric score for sorting (higher = more important).\n    pub fn score(&self) -> u32 {\n        match self {\n            Self::Critical => 100,\n            Self::High => 75,\n            Self::Normal => 50,\n            Self::Low => 25,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for AssignmentPriority {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Critical => write!(f, \"critical\"),\n            Self::High => write!(f, \"high\"),\n            Self::Normal => write!(f, \"normal\"),\n            Self::Low => write!(f, \"low\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::str::FromStr for AssignmentPriority {\n    type Err = String;\n\n    fn from_str(s: &str) -> std::result::Result<Self, Self::Err> {\n        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n            \"critical\" => Ok(Self::Critical),\n            \"high\" => Ok(Self::High),\n            \"normal\" => Ok(Self::Normal),\n            \"low\" => Ok(Self::Low),\n            _ => Err(format!(\"unknown priority: {}\", s)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Status of a bead assignment.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum AssignmentStatus {\n    /// Bead is unassigned\n    Unassigned,\n    /// Bead is assigned to a user\n    Assigned,\n    /// Bead is actively being worked on\n    InProgress,\n    /// Bead work is complete (pending review)\n    Completed,\n    /// Bead is blocked\n    Blocked,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for AssignmentStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Unassigned => write!(f, \"unassigned\"),\n            Self::Assigned => write!(f, \"assigned\"),\n            Self::InProgress => write!(f, \"in_progress\"),\n            Self::Completed => write!(f, \"completed\"),\n            Self::Blocked => write!(f, \"blocked\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::str::FromStr for AssignmentStatus {\n    type Err = String;\n\n    fn from_str(s: &str) -> std::result::Result<Self, Self::Err> {\n        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n            \"unassigned\" => Ok(Self::Unassigned),\n            \"assigned\" => Ok(Self::Assigned),\n            \"in_progress\" | \"in-progress\" => Ok(Self::InProgress),\n            \"completed\" => Ok(Self::Completed),\n            \"blocked\" => Ok(Self::Blocked),\n            _ => Err(format!(\"unknown status: {}\", s)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl BeadAssignment {\n    /// Create a new unassigned bead record.\n    pub fn new(bead_id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            assigned_to: None,\n            assigned_at: None,\n            assigned_by: None,\n            priority: AssignmentPriority::Normal,\n            status: AssignmentStatus::Unassigned,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new assigned bead record.\n    pub fn assigned(\n        bead_id: impl Into<String>,\n        to_user: impl Into<String>,\n        by_user: impl Into<String>,\n        priority: AssignmentPriority,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            bead_id: bead_id.into(),\n            assigned_to: Some(to_user.into()),\n            assigned_at: Some(Utc::now()),\n            assigned_by: Some(by_user.into()),\n            priority,\n            status: AssignmentStatus::Assigned,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this bead is assigned to a specific user.\n    pub fn is_assigned_to(&self, user_id: &str) -> bool {\n        self.assigned_to.as_deref() == Some(user_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this bead is unassigned.\n    pub fn is_unassigned(&self) -> bool {\n        self.assigned_to.is_none() || self.status == AssignmentStatus::Unassigned\n    }\n\n    /// Mark this bead as in progress.\n    pub fn mark_in_progress(&mut self, user_id: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.assigned_to = Some(user_id.into());\n        self.assigned_at = Some(Utc::now());\n        self.status = AssignmentStatus::InProgress;\n    }\n\n    /// Mark this bead as completed.\n    pub fn mark_completed(&mut self) {\n        self.status = AssignmentStatus::Completed;\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign this bead.\n    pub fn unassign(&mut self) {\n        self.assigned_to = None;\n        self.assigned_at = None;\n        self.assigned_by = None;\n        self.status = AssignmentStatus::Unassigned;\n    }\n}\n\n/// Manager for bead assignments in team collaboration mode.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AssignmentManager {\n    assignments: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, BeadAssignment>>>,\n    storage_path: Option<PathBuf>,\n}\n\nimpl AssignmentManager {\n    /// Create a new in-memory assignment manager.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            assignments: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),\n            storage_path: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new assignment manager with file-backed storage.\n    pub fn with_storage<P: AsRef<Path>>(path: P) -> Result<Self> {\n        let storage_path = path.as_ref().to_path_buf();\n        let mut manager = Self {\n            assignments: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),\n            storage_path: Some(storage_path.clone()),\n        };\n\n        // Load existing assignments if file exists\n        if storage_path.exists() {\n            manager.load()?;\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            path = %storage_path.display(),\n            \"Assignment manager initialized with storage\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(manager)\n    }\n\n    /// Assign a bead to a user.\n    pub fn assign(\n        &self,\n        bead_id: impl Into<String>,\n        to_user: impl Into<String>,\n        by_user: impl Into<String>,\n        priority: AssignmentPriority,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let bead_id = bead_id.into();\n        let to_user = to_user.into();\n        let by_user = by_user.into();\n\n        let mut assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let assignment = BeadAssignment::assigned(\n            bead_id.clone(),\n            to_user.clone(),\n            by_user.clone(),\n            priority,\n        );\n\n        assignments.insert(bead_id.clone(), assignment.clone());\n\n        info!(\n            bead_id = %bead_id,\n            to_user = %to_user,\n            by_user = %by_user,\n            priority = %priority,\n            \"Assigned bead to user\"\n        );\n\n        // Persist if storage is configured\n        if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n            self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Unassign a bead.\n    pub fn unassign(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>, by_user: impl Into<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        let bead_id = bead_id.into();\n        let by_user = by_user.into();\n\n        let mut assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        if let Some(assignment) = assignments.get_mut(&bead_id) {\n            assignment.unassign();\n\n            info!(\n                bead_id = %bead_id,\n                by_user = %by_user,\n                \"Unassigned bead\"\n            );\n\n            // Persist if storage is configured\n            if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n                self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\n                \"bead not found: {}\",\n                bead_id\n            )))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Mark a bead as in progress.\n    pub fn mark_in_progress(\n        &self,\n        bead_id: impl Into<String>,\n        user_id: impl Into<String>,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let bead_id = bead_id.into();\n        let user_id = user_id.into();\n\n        let mut assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        if let Some(assignment) = assignments.get_mut(&bead_id) {\n            assignment.mark_in_progress(&user_id);\n\n            debug!(\n                bead_id = %bead_id,\n                user_id = %user_id,\n                \"Marked bead as in progress\"\n            );\n\n            // Persist if storage is configured\n            if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n                self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            // Create a new assignment if it doesn't exist\n            let mut assignment = BeadAssignment::new(bead_id.clone());\n            assignment.mark_in_progress(&user_id);\n            assignments.insert(bead_id.clone(), assignment);\n\n            // Persist if storage is configured\n            if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n                self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Mark a bead as completed.\n    pub fn mark_completed(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        let bead_id = bead_id.into();\n\n        let mut assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        if let Some(assignment) = assignments.get_mut(&bead_id) {\n            assignment.mark_completed();\n\n            info!(\n                bead_id = %bead_id,\n                \"Marked bead as completed\"\n            );\n\n            // Persist if storage is configured\n            if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n                self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\n                \"bead not found: {}\",\n                bead_id\n            )))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get assignment for a specific bead.\n    pub fn get_assignment(&self, bead_id: &str) -> Result<Option<BeadAssignment>> {\n        let assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(assignments.get(bead_id).cloned())\n    }\n\n    /// Get all assignments.\n    pub fn get_all_assignments(&self) -> Result<Vec<BeadAssignment>> {\n        let assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(assignments.values().cloned().collect())\n    }\n\n    /// Get assignments for a specific user.\n    pub fn get_user_assignments(&self, user_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<BeadAssignment>> {\n        let assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let user_assignments: Vec<_> = assignments\n            .values()\n            .filter(|a| a.is_assigned_to(user_id))\n            .cloned()\n            .collect();\n\n        Ok(user_assignments)\n    }\n\n    /// Get unassigned beads sorted by priority.\n    pub fn get_unassigned(&self) -> Result<Vec<BeadAssignment>> {\n        let assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let mut unassigned: Vec<_> = assignments\n            .values()\n            .filter(|a| a.is_unassigned())\n            .cloned()\n            .collect();\n\n        // Sort by priority (descending)\n        unassigned.sort_by(|a, b| b.priority.score().cmp(&a.priority.score()));\n\n        Ok(unassigned)\n    }\n\n    /// Get assignments by status.\n    pub fn get_by_status(&self, status: AssignmentStatus) -> Result<Vec<BeadAssignment>> {\n        let assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let filtered: Vec<_> = assignments\n            .values()\n            .filter(|a| a.status == status)\n            .cloned()\n            .collect();\n\n        Ok(filtered)\n    }\n\n    /// Set priority for a bead.\n    pub fn set_priority(\n        &self,\n        bead_id: impl Into<String>,\n        priority: AssignmentPriority,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let bead_id = bead_id.into();\n\n        let mut assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        if let Some(assignment) = assignments.get_mut(&bead_id) {\n            assignment.priority = priority;\n\n            debug!(\n                bead_id = %bead_id,\n                priority = %priority,\n                \"Set bead priority\"\n            );\n\n            // Persist if storage is configured\n            if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n                self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            Err(ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\n                \"bead not found: {}\",\n                bead_id\n            )))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Remove a bead from assignments (when closed/deleted).\n    pub fn remove_assignment(&self, bead_id: impl Into<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        let bead_id = bead_id.into();\n\n        let mut assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        assignments.remove(&bead_id);\n\n        debug!(bead_id = %bead_id, \"Removed bead assignment\");\n\n        // Persist if storage is configured\n        if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n            self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Load assignments from storage file.\n    fn load(&mut self) -> Result<()> {\n        let path = self.storage_path.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(\"no storage path configured\")\n        })?;\n\n        let content = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| ForgeError::io(\n            \"read\",\n            path,\n            e,\n        ))?;\n\n        let loaded: HashMap<String, BeadAssignment> =\n            serde_yaml::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| ForgeError::YamlParse {\n                context: format!(\"assignments file at {:?}\", path),\n                message: e.to_string(),\n            })?;\n\n        let mut assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        *assignments = loaded;\n\n        info!(\n            path = %path.display(),\n            count = assignments.len(),\n            \"Loaded assignments from storage\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Save assignments to storage file (inner version that doesn't need mut self).\n    fn save_inner(\n        &self,\n        assignments: &HashMap<String, BeadAssignment>,\n        path: &Path,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        if let Some(parent) = path.parent() {\n            fs::create_dir_all(parent)\n                .map_err(|e| ForgeError::io(\"create_dir_all\", parent, e))?;\n        }\n\n        let yaml = serde_yaml::to_string(assignments).map_err(|e| ForgeError::YamlParse {\n            context: \"assignments serialization\".to_string(),\n            message: e.to_string(),\n        })?;\n\n        fs::write(path, yaml)\n            .map_err(|e| ForgeError::io(\"write\", path, e))?;\n\n        debug!(\n            path = %path.display(),\n            count = assignments.len(),\n            \"Saved assignments to storage\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Force save assignments to storage.\n    pub fn save(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        if let Some(ref path) = self.storage_path {\n            self.save_inner(&assignments, path)?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get assignment statistics.\n    pub fn stats(&self) -> Result<AssignmentStats> {\n        let assignments = self.assignments.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::assignment_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let mut stats = AssignmentStats::default();\n\n        for assignment in assignments.values() {\n            stats.total += 1;\n\n            match assignment.status {\n                AssignmentStatus::Unassigned => stats.unassigned += 1,\n                AssignmentStatus::Assigned => stats.assigned += 1,\n                AssignmentStatus::InProgress => stats.in_progress += 1,\n                AssignmentStatus::Completed => stats.completed += 1,\n                AssignmentStatus::Blocked => stats.blocked += 1,\n            }\n\n            // Count by user\n            if let Some(ref user) = assignment.assigned_to {\n                *stats.by_user.entry(user.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(stats)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for AssignmentManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Statistics about bead assignments.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct AssignmentStats {\n    pub total: usize,\n    pub unassigned: usize,\n    pub assigned: usize,\n    pub in_progress: usize,\n    pub completed: usize,\n    pub blocked: usize,\n    pub by_user: HashMap<String, usize>,\n}\n\n// Add assignment_error method to ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n310\t        })?;\n311\t\n312\t        // Update idle status before returning\n313\t        let mut result = Vec::new();\n314\t        for session in sessions.values() {\n315\t            let mut s = session.clone();\n316\t            s.check_idle(self.idle_timeout_seconds);\n317\t            result.push(s);\n318\t    Ok(match action {\n353\t            SessionAction::SpawnWorker => session.role.can_spawn_workers(),\n354\t            SessionAction::KillWorker => session.role.can_kill_workers(),\n355\t self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n364\t            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n365\t        })?;\n366\t\n367\t        let initial_count = 0 {\n372\t            info!(removed, \"Cleaned up stale sessions\");\n373\t        }\n374\t\n375\t        Ok(removed)\n376\t    }\n377\t\n378\t    /// Get session count.\n379\t    pub fn User session types and management for team collaboration.\n//!\n//! This module provides types and functionality for managing multiple users\n//! connected to a FORGE instance, including role-based access control and\n//! session tracking.\n\nuse crate::{ForgeError, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::Instant;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn};\n\n/// User role determining permissions in team collaboration mode.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum UserRole {\n    /// Administrator - full access to all operations\n    Admin,\n    /// Operator - can perform worker operations and manage tasks\n    Operator,\n    /// Viewer - read-only access to dashboard\n    Viewer,\n}\n\nimpl UserRole {\n    /// Check if this role can perform worker spawn operations.\n    pub fn can_spawn_workers(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Admin | Self::Operator)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this role can perform worker kill operations.\n    pub fn can_kill_workers(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Admin | Self::Operator)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this role can modify configuration.\n    pub fn can_modify_config(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Admin)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this role can assign beads/tasks.\n    pub fn can_assign_beads(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Admin | Self::Operator)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this role can kill other users' sessions.\n    pub fn can_manage_sessions(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Admin)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this role can manage users (add/remove users).\n    pub fn can_manage_users(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Self::Admin)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the display name for this role.\n    pub fn display_name(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Admin => \"Admin\",\n            Self::Operator => \"Operator\",\n            Self::Viewer => \"Viewer\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for UserRole {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        write!(f, \"{}\", self.display_name())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::str::FromStr for UserRole {\n    type Err = String;\n\n    fn from_str(s: &str) -> std::result::Result<Self, Self::Err> {\n        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n            \"admin\" => Ok(Self::Admin),\n            \"operator\" => Ok(Self::Operator),\n            \"viewer\" => Ok(Self::Viewer),\n            _ => Err(format!(\"unknown role: {}\", s)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Status of a user session.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum SessionStatus {\n    /// Session is actively receiving updates\n    Active,\n    /// Session is idle (no recent activity)\n    Idle,\n    /// Session has disconnected\n    Disconnected,\n}\n\nimpl SessionStatus {\n    /// Get the display indicator for this status.\n    pub fn indicator(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Active => \"●\",\n            Self::Idle => \"○\",\n            Self::Disconnected => \"○\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for SessionStatus {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Active => write!(f, \"active\"),\n            Self::Idle => write!(f, \"idle\"),\n            Self::Disconnected => write!(f, \"disconnected\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Information about a connected user session.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct UserSession {\n    /// Unique session ID\n    pub session_id: String,\n    /// User identifier (username, email, etc.)\n    pub user_id: String,\n    /// Display name for the user\n    pub display_name: String,\n    /// User role\n    pub role: UserRole,\n    /// Current session status\n    pub status: SessionStatus,\n    /// When the session was created\n    pub connected_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Last activity timestamp\n    pub last_activity: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Current view the user is viewing\n    pub current_view: Option<String>,\n    /// Client information (hostname, etc.)\n    pub client_info: Option<ClientInfo>,\n}\n\n/// Client information for a session.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ClientInfo {\n    /// Client hostname or IP\n    pub hostname: String,\n    /// Client application/version\n    pub client: String,\n    /// Terminal size (if available)\n    pub terminal_size: Option<(u16, u16)>,\n}\n\nimpl UserSession {\n    /// Create a new user session.\n    pub fn new(\n        session_id: impl Into<String>,\n        user_id: impl Into<String>,\n        display_name: impl Into<String>,\n        role: UserRole,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        Self {\n            session_id: session_id.into(),\n            user_id: user_id.into(),\n            display_name: display_name.into(),\n            role,\n            status: SessionStatus::Active,\n            connected_at: now,\n            last_activity: now,\n            current_view: None,\n            client_info: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update the session's last activity timestamp.\n    pub fn update_activity(&mut self) {\n        self.last_activity = Utc::now();\n        self.status = SessionStatus::Active;\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the session as idle based on timeout.\n    pub fn check_idle(&mut self, idle_timeout_seconds: i64) {\n        let idle_duration = Utc::now() - self.last_activity;\n        if idle_duration.num_seconds() > idle_timeout_seconds {\n            self.status = SessionStatus::Idle;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the current view for this session.\n    pub fn set_view(&mut self, view: impl Into<String>) {\n        self.current_view = Some(view.into());\n        self.update_activity();\n    }\n\n    /// Set client information.\n    pub fn set_client_info(&mut self, info: ClientInfo) {\n        self.client_info = Some(info);\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the session is stale (should be disconnected).\n    pub fn is_stale(&self, stale_timeout_seconds: i64) -> bool {\n        let idle_duration = Utc::now() - self.last_activity;\n        idle_duration.num_seconds() > stale_timeout_seconds && self.status == SessionStatus::Disconnected\n    }\n}\n\n/// Manager for user sessions in team collaboration mode.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct SessionManager {\n    sessions: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, UserSession>>>,\n    idle_timeout_seconds: i64,\n    stale_timeout_seconds: i64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for SessionManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SessionManager {\n    /// Create a new session manager with default timeouts.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),\n            idle_timeout_seconds: 300, // 5 minutes\n            stale_timeout_seconds: 3600, // 1 hour\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new session manager with custom timeouts.\n    pub fn with_timeouts(idle_timeout_seconds: i64, stale_timeout_seconds: i64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),\n            idle_timeout_seconds,\n            stale_timeout_seconds,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new user session.\n    pub fn register_session(&self, session: UserSession) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let session_id = session.session_id.clone();\n        let user_id = session.user_id.clone();\n\n        sessions.insert(session_id.clone(), session);\n\n        info!(\n            session_id = %session_id,\n            user_id = %user_id,\n            \"Registered user session\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a session (user disconnects).\n    pub fn unregister_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Result<Option<UserSession>> {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let session = sessions.remove(session_id);\n\n        if let Some(ref s) = session {\n            info!(\n                session_id = %session_id,\n                user_id = %s.user_id,\n                \"Unregistered user session\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(session)\n    }\n\n    /// Update activity for a session.\n    pub fn update_activity(&self, session_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n            session.update_activity();\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update the current view for a session.\n    pub fn update_view(&self, session_id: &str, view: impl Into<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        if let Some(session) = sessions.get_mut(session_id) {\n            session.set_view(view);\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get all active sessions.\n    pub fn get_sessions(&self) -> Result<Vec<UserSession>> {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        // Update idle status before returning\n        let mut result = Vec::new();\n        for session in sessions.values() {\n            let mut s = session.clone();\n            s.check_idle(self.idle_timeout_seconds);\n            result.push(s);\n        }\n\n        Ok(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a specific session by ID.\n    pub fn get_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Result<Option<UserSession>> {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(sessions.get(session_id).cloned())\n    }\n\n    /// Get sessions for a specific user.\n    pub fn get_user_sessions(&self, user_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<UserSession>> {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let user_sessions: Vec<_> = sessions\n            .values()\n            .filter(|s| s.user_id == user_id)\n            .cloned()\n            .collect();\n\n        Ok(user_sessions)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a user has permission to perform an action.\n    pub fn check_permission(&self, session_id: &str, action: SessionAction) -> Result<bool> {\n        let session = self.get_session(session_id)?\n            .ok_or_else(|| ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"session not found: {}\", session_id)))?;\n\n        Ok(match action {\n            SessionAction::SpawnWorker => session.role.can_spawn_workers(),\n            SessionAction::KillWorker => session.role.can_kill_workers(),\n            SessionAction::ModifyConfig => session.role.can_modify_config(),\n            SessionAction::AssignBead => session.role.can_assign_beads(),\n            SessionAction::ManageSessions => session.role.can_manage_sessions(),\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up stale sessions.\n    pub fn cleanup_stale(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let mut sessions = self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        let initial_count = sessions.len();\n        sessions.retain(|_, session| !session.is_stale(self.stale_timeout_seconds));\n        let removed = initial_count - sessions.len();\n\n        if removed > 0 {\n            info!(removed, \"Cleaned up stale sessions\");\n        }\n\n        Ok(removed)\n    }\n\n    /// Get session count.\n    pub fn session_count(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let sessions = self.sessions.lock().map_err(|e| {\n            ForgeError::session_error(format!(\"failed to acquire lock: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(sessions.len())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if multi-user mode is active (any sessions).\n    pub fn is_multi_user(&self) -> Result<bool> {\n        Ok(self.session_count()? > 0)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Actions that require permission checks.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum SessionAction {\n    SpawnWorker,\n    KillWorker,\n    ModifyConfig,\n    AssignBead,\n    ManageSessions,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for SessionAction {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::SpawnWorker => write!(f, \"spawn_worker\"),\n            Self::KillWorker => write!(f, \"kill_worker\"),\n            Self::ModifyConfig => write!(f, \"modify_config\"),\n            Self::AssignBead => write!(f, \"assign_bead\"),\n            Self::ManageSessions => write!(f, \"manage_sessions\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_user_role_permissions() {\n        assert!(UserRole::Admin.can_spawn_workers());\n        assert!(UserRole::Admin.can_kill_workers());\n        assert!(UserRole::Admin.can_modify_config());\n        assert!(UserRole::Admin.can_assign_beads());\n        assert!(UserRole::Admin.can_manage_sessions());\n\n        assert!(UserRole::Operator.can_spawn_workers());\n        assert!(UserRole::Operator.can_kill_workers());\n        assert!(!UserRole::Operator.can_modify_config());\n        assert!(UserRole::Operator.can_assign_beads());\n        assert!(!UserRole::Operator.can_manage_sessions());\n\n        assert!(!UserRole::Viewer.can_spawn_workers());\n        assert!(!UserRole::Viewer.can_kill_workers());\n        assert!(!UserRole::Viewer.can_modify_config());\n        assert!(!UserRole::Viewer.can_assign_beads());\n        assert!(!UserRole::Viewer.can_manage_sessions());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_user_role_from_str() {\n        assert_eq!(\"admin\".parse::<UserRole>().unwrap(), UserRole::Admin);\n        assert_eq!(\"operator\".parse::<UserRole>().unwrap(), UserRole::Operator);\n        assert_eq!(\"viewer\".parse::<UserRole>().unwrap(), UserRole::Viewer);\n        assert!(\"unknown\".parse::<UserRole>().is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_user_session_creation() {\n        let session = UserSession::new(\"test-session\", \"user1\", \"Test User\", UserRole::Operator);\n\n        assert_eq!(session.session_id, \"test-session\");\n        assert_eq!(session.user_id, \"user1\");\n        assert_eq!(session.display_name, \"Test User\");\n        assert_eq!(session.role, UserRole::Operator);\n        assert_eq!(session.status, SessionStatus::Active);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_session_manager() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        let session = UserSession::new(\"s1\", \"user1\", \"User One\", UserRole::Admin);\n        manager.register_session(session).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(manager.session_count().unwrap(), 1);\n\n        let sessions = manager.get_sessions().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(sessions.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(sessions[0].user_id, \"user1\");\n\n        manager.unregister_session(\"s1\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(manager.session_count().unwrap(), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_permission_check() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        let admin_session = UserSession::new(\"admin\", \"admin\", \"Admin\", UserRole::Admin);\n        let viewer_session = UserSession::new(\"viewer\", \"viewer\", \"Viewer\", UserRole::Viewer);\n\n        manager.register_session(admin_session).unwrap();\n        manager.register_session(viewer_session).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(manager.check_permission(\"admin\", SessionAction::ModifyConfig).unwrap());\n        assert!(!manager.check_permission(\"viewer\", SessionAction::ModifyConfig).unwrap());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_session_idle_check() {\n        let mut session = UserSession::new(\"s1\", \"user1\", \"User\", UserRole::Viewer);\n        assert_eq!(session.status, SessionStatus::Active);\n\n        // Set last activity to 10 minutes ago\n        session.last_activity = Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::minutes(10);\n        session.check_idle(300); // 5 minute timeout\n\n        assert_eq!(session.status, SessionStatus::Idle);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_session_view_update() {\n        let manager = SessionManager::new();\n\n        let session = UserSession::new(\"s1\", \"user1\", \"User\", UserRole::Viewer);\n        manager.register_session(session).unwrap();\n\n        manager.update_view(\"s1\", \"Workers\").unwrap();\n\n        let retrieved = manager.get_session(\"s1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.current_view, Some(\"Workers\".to_string()));\n    }\n}\n","numLines":515,"startLine":1,"totalLines":515}}}
