Ctrl+U triggers update\\n2. Cargo build succeeds\\n3. cp fails with 'Text file busy'\\n4. Error shown in TUI but update doesn't complete\\n\\n## Investigation\\n\\nSee docs/investigations/fg-2pe-ctrl-u-investigation.md for full analysis.\\n\\n## Recommended Fix\\n\\nThe self_update.rs module already has the correct approach:\\n1. Download new binary to staging location\\n2. On restart, rename staging binary to final location\\n3. Show 'Update ready - restart required' message\\n\\nThe issue is that the current running binary is at ~/.cargo/bin/forge and cannot be overwritten.\\n\\n## Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n- [ ] Update shows clear status during download/build\\n- [ ] Update completes successfully even when forge is running\\n- [ ] User sees 'Update ready - restart required' message\\n- [ ] Restart applies the update correctly\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- docs/investigations/fg-2pe-ctrl-u-investigation.md\\n- crates/forge-core/src/self_update.rs\\n- update-forge.sh\",\"design\":\"\",\"acceptance_criteria\":\"\",\"notes\":\"\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":0,\"issue_type\":\"bug\",\"assignee\":\"claude-code-glm-4.7-echo\",\"owner\":\"\",\"created_at\":\"2026-02-18T05:51:09.766674030Z\",\"created_by\":\"coder\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-28T18:29:40.936641485Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-28T18:29:40.936578096Z\",\"close_reason\":\"## Summary of work completed\\n\\nThe 'Text file busy' error when updating forge has been **fully resolved**. The fix was implemented in commits:\\n- `aec5f73` - Fixed env var propagation and staging path issues\\n- `7ffe04e` - Fixed missing should_quit to trigger restart\\n\\n## How the fix works\\n\\nThe self-update flow now correctly avoids overwriting the running binary:\\n\\n1. **Download to staging**: `perform_update()` downloads the new binary to `/tmp/forge-update/forge-staged` (not the final location)\\n2. **Trigger restart**: After successful download, `should_quit = true` is set, causing the main loop to exit\\n3. **Exec staging binary**: `restart_with_new_binary()` sets env vars (FORGE_AUTO_RESTART, FORGE_INSTALL_PATH, FORGE_STAGING_PATH) and execs the staging binary\\n4. **Self-install on startup**: The new binary detects the restart env vars in `check_and_perform_self_install()` and installs itself to `~/.cargo/bin/forge`\\n\\n## Key fixes from aec5f73\\n\\n1. **Env vars set directly**: Used `std::env::set_var()` instead of setting on a Command object (which was ignored by execv())\\n2. **Stable staging path**: Changed from PID-based filename to stable `/tmp/forge-update/forge-staged` so manual restarts work\\n3. **Manual restart detection**: Added fallback to detect stale staging files for users who quit instead of auto-restarting\\n\\n## Retrospective\\n\\n- **What worked:** The atomic staging + exec approach completely avoids the ETXTBSY error. The running binary is never overwritten—instead, we exec a new binary that installs itself on startup.\\n- **What didn't:** The original cargo build + cp approach was fundamentally flawed because it tried to overwrite a running executable. The self-update module had the correct design but had bugs (env var propagation, unstable staging path).\\n- **Surprise:** The `unsafe` block around `env::set_var()` is actually correct here—exec() replaces the entire process image, so no other thread can observe the transient env state.\\n- **Reusable pattern:** For self-updating binaries on Linux: download to staging → exec staging → new binary installs itself on startup. This works because rename() is atomic and the new process isn't \\\"running\\\" from the target location yet.\",\"closed_by_session\":\"\",\"source_system\":\"\",\"source_repo\":\".\",\"deleted_by\":\"\",\"delete_reason\":\"\",\"original_type\":\"\",\"compaction_level\":0,\"original_size\":0,\"sender\":\"\",\"labels\":[\"P0\",\"bug\",\"deferred\",\"failure-count:33\",\"update\"]}\n{\"id\":\"bd-4d2i4\",\"title\":\"Fix test_monthly_costs failing when test runs near month boundary\",\"description\":\"## Problem\\nThe forge-cost integration test `test_monthly_costs` is currently failing with:\\n  left: 2\\n  right: 3\\n\\nThe test inserts 3 API calls: today, 1 day ago, and 2 days ago. {\"id\":\"bd-3er6\",\"title\":\"Add cleanup of spawned worker sessions in test-forge-workers.sh\",\"description\":\"## Problem\\n\\nThe test-forge-workers.sh test spawns worker sessions via keyboard shortcuts (g/s/o/h keys) but only cleans up the main test session, leaving orphaned worker sessions like:\\n- forge-forge-glm-4_7-*\\n- forge-forge-sonnet-*\\n- forge-forge-opus-*\\n- forge-forge-haiku-*\\n\\nThese sessions remain running with 'sleep 300' commands, accumulating over time.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nLines 80-100 in test-forge-workers.sh send worker spawn keys (g/s/o/h) which create worker tmux sessions. But line 118 only calls stop_forge for the test session, not the spawned workers.\\n\\n## Solution\\n\\nAdd cleanup function that:\\n1. Captures list of tmux sessions before test starts\\n2. After test completes, identifies new sessions matching 'forge-forge-*' pattern\\n3. Kills all spawned worker sessions\\n4. Calls stop_forge for main test session\\n\\n## Implementation\\n\\n```bash\\n# In test-forge-workers.sh, before spawning workers:\\nbefore_sessions=$(tmux list-sessions 2>/dev/null | grep '^forge-forge' | cut -d: -f1 || true)\\n\\n# After test completes:\\ncleanup_spawned_workers() {\\n    local before=\\\"$1\\\"\\n    local after=$(tmux list-sessions 2>/dev/null | grep '^forge-forge' | cut -d: -f1 || true)\\n    \\n    # Kill new sessions\\n    while IFS= read -r session; do\\n        if \\\\! echo \\\"$before\\\" | grep -q \\\"^$session$\\\"; then\\n            log_info \\\"Cleaning up spawned worker: $session\\\"\\n            tmux kill-session -t \\\"$session\\\" 2>/dev/null || true\\n        fi\\n    done <<< \\\"$after\\\"\\n}\\n\\ncleanup_spawned_workers \\\"$before_sessions\\\"\\nstop_forge \\\"$session\\\"\\n```\\n\\n## Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n- [ ] Test captures session list before spawning workers\\n- [ ] Test kills all forge-forge-* sessions created during test\\n- [ ] No orphaned sessions remain after test completes\\n- [ ] Test still passes all assertions\\n- [ ] Cleanup happens even if test fails (trap handler)\",\"design\":\"\",\"acceptance_criteria\":\"\",\"notes\":\"\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":0,\"issue_type\":\"bug\",\"assignee\":\"claude-code-glm-4.7-echo\",\"owner\":\"\",\"created_at\":\"2026-02-13T22:03:14.743851798Z\",\"created_by\":\"coder\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-28T18:19:54.027286032Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-28T18:19:54.027182764Z\",\"close_reason\":\"Fixed worker session cleanup regex pattern to properly match forge-forge-* session names.\\n\\n## Retrospective\\n- **What worked:** The cleanup infrastructure was already in place in test-helpers.sh with cleanup_spawned_workers() and capture_before_sessions() functions. Only the regex pattern needed updating to match the double-prefix session naming format.\\n- **What didn't:** The original pattern did not account for sessions like forge-forge-glm-4_7-* because it only expected a single forge- prefix.\\n- **Surprise:** The test framework already had comprehensive cleanup logic including a trap handler for script exit.\\n- **Reusable pattern:** When matching patterns that may have optional prefixes, use regex with optional groups to handle variations.\",\"closed_by_session\":\"\",\"source_system\":\"\",\"source_repo\":\".\",\"deleted_by\":\"\",\"delete_reason\":\"\",\"original_type\":\"\",\"compaction_level\":0,\"original_size\":0,\"sender\":\"\",\"labels\":[\"awaiting-human\",\"bug\",\"cleanup\",\"escalated\",\"failure-count:2\",\"human-review-needed\",\"testing\",\"timed-out\"],\"comments\":[{\"id\":5,\"issue_id\":\"bd-3er6\",\"author\":\"coder\",\"text\":\"⏰ **TIMEOUT ESCALATION**: Timeout after 1800s -
